sonnet_review.biasedText: It's freezing. It's terrifying. And it's potential deadly.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of the subheadline's emotive and sensational language in the author's own voice.

sonnet_review.biasedText: secret police coming to abduct them, disappear them
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of the author characterizing ICE as 'secret police' who 'abduct' and 'disappear' people, using loaded partisan language.
Matches my detection of the explicitly derogatory term 'bastards' used in the author's own voice to refer to ICE agents.

sonnet_review.biasedText: violent entertainment for a ravenous national audience
sonnet_review.reason: While I focused on 'atrocities' in the same passage, 'ravenous' is also emotionally loaded language that goes beyond neutral reporting. A defensible additional detection within the same passage.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The article extensively covers anti-ICE protesters' perspectives, emotions, and motivations while providing no perspective from ICE, law enforcement, or anyone supporting immigration enforcement.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of bias by omission. The entire article presents only the anti-ICE perspective with no counterbalancing viewpoint.

sonnet_review.biasedText: ICE is here, and ICE has killed, and ICE may kill again.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of this dramatic, unqualified claim presented as fact in the author's own voice without citing specifics.

gpt_review.biasedText: Immigration and Customs Enforcement moves fast in Minneapolis, the way you have to when an entire city is against you.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the unsubstantiated generalization that 'an entire city is against' ICE.

gpt_review.biasedText: Just as roadkill summons vultures, so too does human misery summon journalists, who travel across the country to shove cameras and recorders at people enduring things no one should experience.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the graphic, emotionally charged metaphor and normative claims.

gpt_review.biasedText: But the line between journalism and pornography grows vanishingly thin at times like these.
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this specific sentence separately, but it is part of the same emotionally charged passage and uses sensational language ('pornography') in the author's own voice. A defensible detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: Newsworthy tales of ICE atrocities can easily become violent entertainment for a ravenous national audience; action-movie sequences carried out by caricatures, grief and trauma commodified for mass consumption.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the author using 'atrocities' and other subjective judgments in their own voice.

gpt_review.biasedText: The world might breathe a sigh of relief, but Minneapolis can't afford it. ICE is here, and ICE has killed, and ICE may kill again.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of this dramatic, unqualified claim presented as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: Here the city stands: residents with whistles standing against men with guns and flak vests.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the David-vs-Goliath framing to emotionally heighten the narrative.

gpt_review.biasedText: Whatever the bastards had planned for the apartment complex will have to wait for another day.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the derogatory term 'bastards' in the author's narrative voice.

gpt_review.biasedText: The article provides detailed, sympathetic portrayals of activists' motivations and risks, while including only negative characterizations of ICE (e.g., 'ICE has killed'), without context or any explanation for ICE actions or policy perspective.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of bias by omission — the complete absence of any ICE, law enforcement, or pro-enforcement perspective.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Immigration and Customs Enforcement moves fast in Minneapolis, the way you have to when an entire city is against you.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this unsubstantiated generalization about an 'entire city' being against ICE, presented as fact.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Just as roadkill summons vultures, so too does human misery summon journalists, who travel across the country to shove cameras and recorders at people enduring things no one should experience.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this highly emotionally charged metaphor and normative claim in the author's own voice.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: A sudden vision of myself in the future, talking to grand-nieces and -nephews: Yes, I had a chance to warn people about the secret police coming to abduct them, disappear them, or release them into this dangerous cold without a coat or cell phone, but journalistic ethics, you see …
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: While Sonnet caught the 'secret police' language, it missed the broader framing of this passage where the author rationalizes abandoning journalistic neutrality.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Hell with it. I take the whistle.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the author explicitly abandoning journalistic neutrality and joining the activists, revealing a clear partisan stance.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Police in riot gear stood menacingly in front of the hotel
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the interpretive adverb 'menacingly' that assigns threatening intent to police in the author's own voice.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Here the city stands: residents with whistles standing against men with guns and flak vests.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this dramatic David-vs-Goliath framing in the author's own voice.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: It's freezing. It's terrifying. And it's potential deadly. But anti-ICE activists are not giving up.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the sensational, emotionally charged subheadline language in the author's own voice.
